member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.AddCommand
member.summary:
pcpm add <PACKAGE> — declare a direct dependency. In CPM mode this:
Resolves a version (explicit via --version, otherwise the latest stable from the feed).
Writes the version into Directory.Packages.props.
Adds a <PackageReference Include="X" /> entry to one or more .csproj files.
Runs pcpm install to materialise the change in the store.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.ConvertCommand
member.summary:
pcpm convert — convert a workspace to Central Package Management, or revert from it.
Scans all .csproj/.fsproj/.vbproj files, collects every versioned <PackageReference>, writes the resolved versions to Directory.Packages.props, and strips the Version attribute from each project file. Pass --revert to undo a previous conversion.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.InitCommand
member.summary:
pcpm init — initialise a pcpm workspace at the current directory:
Writes pcpm.json with the default configuration.
Writes Directory.Packages.props with CPM enabled (empty <PackageVersion> list).
If multiple .csproj files exist, also writes pcpm-workspace.yaml.
Idempotent: re-running is safe and does not overwrite user-edited fields it doesn't know about.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.InstallCommand
member.summary: pcpm install — the workhorse. Resolves the full dependency graph, downloads each unique package into the content-addressable store, hardlinks it into ~/.nuget/packages so dotnet restore sees it instantly, and writes pcpm.lock. Finally invokes dotnet restore if the workspace contains a .sln or .csproj at the root.

member.name: M:pcpm.Cli.Commands.InstallCommand.AutoResolveConflictsAsync(pcpm.Core.Abstractions.ResolutionResult,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{pcpm.Core.Models.PackageId,pcpm.Core.Models.PackageDependency},System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Automatically resolves dependency conflicts by pinning each conflicting package in Directory.Packages.props and re-running resolution.
Conflicts happen when a transitive package is discovered (added to constraints) but the resolver couldn't pick a satisfying version — either because the NuGet feed had no results (e.g. paged registration) or because multiple constraints clash.
We loop up to maxRounds times to handle cascading transitives that are themselves discovered only after earlier conflicts are resolved.

member.name: M:pcpm.Cli.Commands.InstallCommand.PickBestVersion(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{pcpm.Core.Models.PackageVersion},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{pcpm.Core.Abstractions.VersionRangeRequest})
member.summary: Picks the highest version from (already sorted newest-first) that satisfies all . Returns null if none qualifies.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.VersionRangeHelper
member.summary: Tiny helper for building VersionRanges with a single pinned version.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.ListCommand
member.summary: pcpm list — list all packages in the current pcpm.lock as a Spectre table.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.OutdatedCommand
member.summary: pcpm outdated — for every package in pcpm.lock, query the feed for the latest stable version and report any that have moved forward.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.RemoveCommand
member.summary: pcpm remove <PACKAGE> — undo add: remove the package from CPM and from every project's <PackageReference> list. Idempotent: removing a package that isn't there is a no-op.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.StoreCommand
member.summary: pcpm store status — show disk usage and count for the global content-addressable store.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Commands.WhyCommand
member.summary: pcpm why <PACKAGE> — show the chain of dependencies that pulled a given package into the lockfile. Walks the lockfile graph and prints a tree.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.Program
member.summary: Entry point. Wires the pcpm services into a plain , then hands control to a Spectre.Console.Cli . We don't use the generic host's Build() because that seals the service collection, which Spectre then trips over when it lazily registers command types during RunAsync. The lazy provider in sidesteps that cleanly.

member.name: T:pcpm.Cli.TypeRegistrar
member.summary: Bridges Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection to Spectre.Console.Cli's . The provider is built lazily inside the resolver — that way Spectre's late Register calls (which happen during RunAsync) keep working even after Build() has been called.
